storylets.title: {=order_guard_post_accept_title}A post to stand
storylets.setup: {=order_guard_post_accept_setup}The serjeant catches your sleeve as the watch changes. "Gate post, till third bell. Eyes outward." He waits on your answer, already looking past you to the next man.

options.text: {=order_guard_post_accept_diligent_text}"Aye, serjeant." You set your feet and keep your eyes on the gate.
options.tooltip: {=order_guard_post_accept_diligent_tt}Stand watch by the book. Lord will notice.

options.text: {=order_guard_post_accept_train_text}You nod. Between rounds you'll run the polearm drills in your head — no one has to know.
{=order_guard_post_accept_slack_text}You take the post. The moment the serjeant's back turns, you lean on your spear.
options.tooltip: {=order_guard_post_accept_slack_tt}Lean on the spear. Scrutiny risk if caught.

options.text: {=order_guard_post_accept_scheme_text}"Aye." *Eyes outward. Purse outward-leaning too, if it comes to that.*
options.tooltip: {=order_guard_post_accept_scheme_tt}Watch for an angle. Possible coin, scrutiny risk.

options.text: {=order_guard_post_accept_decline_text}"Not me tonight. Find another." The serjeant's jaw tightens.

storylets.title: {=ogp_mid_quiet_title}The hour turns
storylets.setup: {=ogp_mid_quiet_setup}Nothing moves at the gate. A dog barks somewhere beyond the wall; another answers. The cold settles into your shoulders and stays there.

options.text: {=ogp_mid_quiet_opt}The hour turns over.

storylets.title: {=ogp_mid_drunk_title}A man at the gate
storylets.setup: {=ogp_mid_drunk_setup}A townsman sways up to the gate well after curfew, wine on his breath and bruises on one hand. He waves a coin at you and asks, not quite begging, to be let in to his wife.

options.text: {=ogp_mid_drunk_opt_turn}Order him back. Curfew is curfew.
{=ogp_mid_drunk_opt_wave}Nod him through. It's one tired man.
{=ogp_mid_drunk_opt_coin}Take the coin. Look the other way.

storylets.title: {=ogp_mid_merchant_title}A wagon on the road
storylets.setup: {=ogp_mid_merchant_setup}A trader's cart rolls up out of the dark, lamp swinging, axle complaining. He's an hour late for the gate — and he knows it. He looks tired enough to pay for a friendly eye.

options.text: {=ogp_mid_merchant_opt_log}Note the name and hour. Pass him through.
{=ogp_mid_merchant_opt_bribe}Accept a "thank-you." Forget his name by morning.
{=ogp_mid_merchant_opt_back}Turn the wagon around. He can wait for dawn.

storylets.title: {=ogp_mid_fellow_title}Rassak at the next post
storylets.setup: {=ogp_mid_fellow_setup}Rassak stands the next post over, leaning on his spear the same as you. He lifts his chin — quiet invitation to trade gripes while nothing's happening. The serjeant is at the far wall.

options.text: {=ogp_mid_fellow_opt_chat}Drift over. Swap a word or two.
{=ogp_mid_fellow_opt_silent}Shake your head once. Back to the gate.

storylets.title: {=ogp_mid_shout_title}A cry from the dark
storylets.setup: {=ogp_mid_shout_setup}Something yells outside the wall — a man's voice, two words you can't make out, cut off sharp. Could be a drunk. Could be something else. You're the nearest post.

options.text: {=ogp_mid_shout_opt_check}Call it up. Look over the wall.
{=ogp_mid_shout_opt_hold}Stay at the post. Someone else's problem.
{=ogp_mid_shout_opt_alone}Step outside. See for yourself.
options.tooltip: {=ogp_mid_shout_opt_alone_tt}Off your post. Scrutiny up if noticed.

storylets.title: {=ogp_res_dil_title}Third bell, post relieved
storylets.setup: {=ogp_res_dil_setup}The relief arrives at third bell exact. The corporal looks at your log, grunts once — which is approval from him — and waves you off the gate. The serjeant is already telling someone else how it ought to be done.

options.text: {=ogp_res_dil_opt}Rack your spear. Walk to the billet.

storylets.title: {=ogp_res_tr_title}The drill in your hands
storylets.setup: {=ogp_res_tr_setup}You walked through the polearm form a hundred times in your head tonight. On the way back to the billet you catch yourself sliding a foot back into the stance without thinking. Something settled in.

options.text: {=ogp_res_tr_opt}Turn in. Tomorrow the hands will remember.

storylets.title: {=ogp_res_sl_title}A long night, mostly leaning
storylets.setup: {=ogp_res_sl_setup}Third bell finally crawls around. Your shoulders ache from the spear you barely carried. The corporal looks at your post-log, looks at you, and doesn't say anything — which is a worse sign than a shout.

options.text: {=ogp_res_sl_opt}Stumble off. Pretend you didn't catch the look.

storylets.title: {=ogp_res_sc_title}Coin in a knotted rag
storylets.setup: {=ogp_res_sc_setup}You count the night's take in the shadow of the billet wall: not bad for a gate post. The knot goes in a boot until you can pass it somewhere safer. No one is looking. No one, if you've read the rotation right.

options.text: {=ogp_res_sc_opt_pocket}Stash the coin. Walk to the billet clean.
{=ogp_res_sc_opt_seen}Fumble the knot. A corporal is behind you — how long?
